title: Dimensionally nilpotent Jordan algebras
journal: Proceedings of the American Mathematical Society
abstract:
An algebra 

  
    A
    A
  

 of dimension 

  
    n
    n
  

 is called dimensionally nilpotent if it has a nilpotent derivation 

  
    ∂
    \partial
  

 with the property that 

  
    
      
        
          ∂
          
            n
            −
            1
          
        
      
      ≠
      0
    
    {\partial ^{n - 1}} \ne 0
  

. Here we show that a dimensionally nilpotent Jordan algebra 

  
    A
    A
  

 over a perfect field of characteristic not 2 or 3 is either (i) nilpotent, or (ii) one-dimensional modulo its maximal nilpotent ideal. This result is also extended to noncommutative Jordan algebras.
